attack_chain.description: Attacker compromised an inactive Drizly employee account (via reused password) to gain initial access.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a hacker breached an employee account

evidence_chunks.quote: a hacker breached an employee account

attack_chain.technique_name: Data from Information Repositories: Code Repositories
attack_chain.description: Attacker accessed Drizly's corporate GitHub repositories using the compromised employee credentials.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: got access to Drizly’s corporate GitHub login information

evidence_chunks.quote: got access to Drizly’s corporate GitHub login information

attack_chain.description: Attacker harvested AWS/database credentials stored in source code on GitHub.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Drizly stored login credentials on GitHub contrary to the platform’s own guidance

evidence_chunks.quote: Drizly stored login credentials on GitHub contrary to the platform’s own guidance

attack_chain.description: Attacker used harvested credentials to access the AWS-hosted database (RDS) containing 2.5 million customer records.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: hacked into the company’s database, and then stole customers’ information

evidence_chunks.quote: hacked into the company’s database, and then stole customers’ information

attack_chain.description: Customer PII was exfiltrated and subsequently offered for sale on dark web marketplaces.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: personal information that Drizly had collected about consumers was offered for sale on two different publicly accessible sites on the dark web

evidence_chunks.quote: personal information that Drizly had collected about consumers was offered for sale on two different publicly accessible sites on the dark web

initial_access_vector: Compromise of an inactive employee account via reused password, leading to access to corporate GitHub where AWS/database credentials were stored in source code.
persistence_mechanism: None described; attacker leveraged valid credentials obtained from GitHub source code.
privilege_escalation: No explicit privilege escalation; attacker used credentials found in source code which already had database access.
